mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending economic contraction, causing equity valuations to contract over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and industrial production confirms robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and INDPRO fail to predict positive 20-day returns with a win rate above 52 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price indices compress real yields and trigger central bank tightening expectations, causing negative short-term equity returns through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL increases by more than 0.3 percent monthly during periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 230.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, supporting higher corporate profit margins and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows or 60-day forward returns show no directional bias.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50000 signals abrupt labor market weakness, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of months where PAYEMS falls by more than 50000.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and real GDP growth confirm broad-based consumer and investment demand, lifting equity valuations through improved earnings forecasts over the next month.
failure_condition: Combined positive shocks to HOUST and GDPC1 fail to generate a statistically significant positive alpha over 20-day horizons in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 1.5 percent, monetary policy tightens sufficiently to dampen economic growth expectations, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 58 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS surpasses 1.5 percent.
